subtopics.title: SQL & querying
subtopics.brief: SQL for analysts: SELECT/WHERE/GROUP BY, joins (inner/left/anti), aggregation, window functions, CTEs, NULL semantics, query correctness pitfalls. Ground in PostgreSQL documentation concepts.

subtopics.title: Spreadsheets & Excel
subtopics.brief: Excel/Google Sheets for analysts: lookups (VLOOKUP/XLOOKUP/INDEX-MATCH), absolute vs relative references, pivot tables, common formula errors, data cleaning, when spreadsheets break down.

subtopics.title: Python for data (pandas)
subtopics.brief: Python for data analysis with pandas: DataFrame vs Series, filtering with boolean masks, groupby aggregations, merge/join types and row-explosion pitfalls, handling NaN, vectorization vs loops, common gotchas (SettingWithCopyWarning, chained indexing).

subtopics.title: Advanced SQL
subtopics.brief: Advanced SQL: window frames and ranking deep-dives, recursive CTEs, query plans and indexes, EXISTS vs IN performance, pivoting, deduplication patterns, transaction isolation basics.

subtopics.title: Power BI
subtopics.brief: Power BI for analysts: data model relationships, DAX measures vs calculated columns, filter context, star schema import, refresh modes (import vs DirectQuery), slicers and drill-through. Microsoft PL-300-style scenarios.

subtopics.title: Tableau
subtopics.brief: Tableau for analysts: dimensions vs measures, LOD expressions intuition, calculated fields, filters order of operations, dashboards vs worksheets, extracts vs live connections, table calculations.

subtopics.title: R for data analysis
subtopics.brief: R fundamentals for analysts: vectors and data frames, dplyr verbs (filter/mutate/group_by/summarise), pipes, factors, NA handling, ggplot2 grammar basics, common beginner errors.

subtopics.title: Descriptive statistics
subtopics.brief: Descriptive statistics: mean vs median under skew, standard deviation, percentiles, distributions, correlation vs causation, sampling basics, misleading summary statistics.

subtopics.title: A/B testing
subtopics.brief: A/B testing for analysts: hypothesis setup, significance and p-values, power and sample size, common pitfalls (peeking, multiple comparisons, novelty effects), interpreting results honestly.

subtopics.title: Statistical inference
subtopics.brief: Statistical inference: interpreting confidence intervals correctly, standard error vs standard deviation, Central Limit Theorem in practice, t-tests, Type I vs Type II errors, sample vs population, statistical vs practical significance.

subtopics.title: Probability essentials
subtopics.brief: Applied probability: conditional probability, independence, Bayes' theorem with base rates (diagnostic-test and fraud-detection scenarios), expected value decisions, binomial vs normal vs Poisson and when each applies.

subtopics.title: Bayesian thinking
subtopics.brief: Bayesian reasoning for practitioners: priors and posteriors intuition, updating beliefs with evidence, base-rate neglect, credible vs confidence intervals, Bayes factors conceptually, when Bayesian beats frequentist framing.

subtopics.title: Causal inference
subtopics.brief: Causal inference for analysts: confounders and colliders, randomization vs observational data, difference-in-differences intuition, instrumental variables conceptually, regression discontinuity, why correlation fails, selection bias.

subtopics.title: Experimental design
subtopics.brief: Designing experiments beyond A/B: blocking and stratification, factorial designs, randomization units and interference, pre-registration, metrics choice, minimum detectable effect, guardrail metrics.

subtopics.title: Survey design & measurement
subtopics.brief: Survey methodology: sampling frames, response and selection bias, leading questions, Likert scales, margin of error, weighting, nonresponse handling, questionnaire pitfalls.

subtopics.title: ML fundamentals
subtopics.brief: Machine learning fundamentals: supervised vs unsupervised learning, classification vs regression tasks, train/validation/test splits, overfitting and underfitting, bias-variance tradeoff, why cross-validation. Coursera Machine Learning-level intuition, scenario-based, no heavy math.

subtopics.title: Feature engineering & data prep
subtopics.brief: Preparing data for models: handling missing values, one-hot vs label encoding, feature scaling and when it matters, target leakage, outlier treatment, class imbalance handling. Practical scenarios an ML practitioner faces.

subtopics.title: Regression essentials
subtopics.brief: Regression for analysts: interpreting linear regression coefficients and intercepts, R-squared meaning and limits, residual patterns, multicollinearity symptoms, when logistic regression applies, odds ratios, extrapolation dangers.

subtopics.title: Model evaluation
subtopics.brief: Evaluating ML models: accuracy limits under class imbalance, precision vs recall tradeoffs, F1, confusion matrices, ROC-AUC vs precision-recall curves, classification threshold selection, probability calibration, validation vs test sets.

subtopics.title: Deep learning basics
subtopics.brief: Neural networks conceptually: layers and activations, why nonlinearity, gradient descent and learning rate intuition, overfitting and dropout, CNNs for images vs RNNs/transformers for sequences, transfer learning, when deep learning is overkill.

subtopics.title: NLP essentials
subtopics.brief: Natural language processing: tokenization, embeddings intuition, TF-IDF vs semantic similarity, named entity recognition, sentiment analysis pitfalls, text classification workflow, evaluation of NLP systems.

subtopics.title: Working with LLMs
subtopics.brief: Using large language models well: prompt structure and few-shot examples, hallucination causes and mitigations, RAG conceptually, context windows, temperature, evaluating LLM outputs, fine-tuning vs prompting vs RAG tradeoffs, responsible use.

subtopics.title: Clustering & unsupervised learning
subtopics.brief: Unsupervised methods: k-means mechanics and limits, choosing k, hierarchical clustering, DBSCAN intuition, dimensionality reduction (PCA) conceptually, cluster validation, common misuse.

subtopics.title: Recommender systems

subtopics.brief: How healthcare works: payer/provider/patient triangle, insurance concepts (premiums, deductibles, networks), fee-for-service vs value-based care, care settings, cost drivers, international system models compared.

subtopics.title: Epidemiology
subtopics.brief: Epi fundamentals: incidence vs prevalence, relative vs absolute risk, sensitivity/specificity and screening tradeoffs, study designs (cohort, case-control, RCT), confounding, outbreak investigation basics, herd immunity.

subtopics.title: Clinical trials
subtopics.brief: Trials: phases I-IV purposes, randomization and blinding, placebo and control arms, endpoints (surrogate vs clinical), intention-to-treat, informed consent, why trials fail, reading trial results critically.

subtopics.title: Medical terminology
subtopics.brief: Medical language: common prefixes/roots/suffixes, body systems vocabulary, abbreviations and their dangers, reading clinical notes basics, lab value conventions, anatomical directions.

subtopics.title: Health data & privacy
subtopics.brief: Health information: HIPAA concepts (PHI, minimum necessary, business associates), de-identification standards, EHR basics, interoperability, patient rights, breach implications, research data use.

subtopics.title: Nutrition science
subtopics.brief: Evidence-based nutrition: macronutrients roles, energy balance honestly, micronutrient basics, reading studies (observational limits), label reading, popular diet claims vs evidence, hydration myths.

subtopics.title: Mental health literacy
subtopics.brief: Mental health fundamentals: stress vs anxiety vs disorders, depression signs, evidence-based treatments overview (therapy types, medication basics), stigma effects, supporting colleagues appropriately, crisis resources awareness. Educational framing.

subtopics.title: Public health
subtopics.brief: Population health: prevention levels (primary/secondary/tertiary), social determinants of health, vaccination programs logic, health disparities measurement, screening program tradeoffs, policy interventions evidence.

subtopics.title: Genetics essentials
subtopics.brief: Genetics literacy: DNA/genes/chromosomes basics, inheritance patterns, genotype vs phenotype, genetic testing types and limits, penetrance, gene-environment interaction, ethics of genetic information.

subtopics.title: Climate science
subtopics.brief: Climate fundamentals: greenhouse effect mechanism, carbon cycle, warming evidence lines, climate vs weather, feedback loops, scenarios and uncertainty honestly, attribution science basics, common misconceptions.

subtopics.title: Renewable energy
subtopics.brief: Energy transition: solar/wind economics and capacity factors, intermittency and storage, grid basics, levelized cost of energy, nuclear's role debates, electrification, energy density tradeoffs.

subtopics.title: ESG & sustainability reporting
subtopics.brief: ESG landscape: E/S/G pillar contents, materiality assessment, reporting frameworks (GRI, SASB, TCFD conceptually), greenwashing red flags, ESG ratings limits, regulatory direction, investor use.

subtopics.title: Carbon accounting
subtopics.brief: Emissions measurement: scopes 1/2/3 definitions and boundaries, emission factors, offsets vs reductions and offset quality problems, net-zero claims scrutiny, carbon pricing mechanisms, supply chain emissions challenges.

subtopics.title: Circular economy
subtopics.brief: Circularity: linear vs circular models, recycling realities and limits, design for disassembly, product-as-service models, rebound effects, lifecycle assessment basics, waste hierarchy.

subtopics.title: Contract fundamentals
subtopics.brief: Contracts (jurisdiction-neutral concepts): offer/acceptance/consideration, written vs oral, key clauses (indemnity, limitation of liability, termination), breach and remedies, boilerplate that matters, red flags. Educational, not legal advice.

subtopics.title: Intellectual property
subtopics.brief: IP basics: patents vs copyrights vs trademarks vs trade secrets, what each protects, work-for-hire, open source licenses (permissive vs copyleft), fair use concepts, IP in employment agreements.

subtopics.title: Data privacy regulation
subtopics.brief: Privacy law landscape: GDPR core concepts (lawful bases, data subject rights, controllers vs processors), CCPA/CPRA basics, consent standards, cross-border transfers, breach notification, privacy by design.

subtopics.title: Business law essentials
subtopics.brief: Business law literacy: entity types (LLC, corporation) tradeoffs, fiduciary duties, liability shields and piercing, securities law basics for startups, employment vs contractor classification, common legal traps.

subtopics.title: Compliance & ethics
subtopics.brief: Corporate compliance: anti-bribery (FCPA concepts), conflicts of interest, insider trading basics, whistleblower protections, code of conduct design, compliance program elements, ethical gray zones.

subtopics.title: Linear algebra
subtopics.brief: Linear algebra intuition: vectors and dot products meaning, matrix multiplication as transformation, linear independence, rank, eigenvalues conceptually, why it matters for ML, solving systems intuition.

subtopics.title: Calculus essentials
subtopics.brief: Calculus concepts: derivative as rate of change, chain rule intuition, optimization via derivatives, integrals as accumulation, partial derivatives, gradients and why ML uses them, common misconceptions.

subtopics.title: Discrete mathematics
subtopics.brief: Discrete math: sets and logic, combinatorics (permutations vs combinations), pigeonhole principle, graph theory basics, induction intuition, modular arithmetic, boolean algebra.

subtopics.title: Optimization
subtopics.brief: Optimization thinking: objective functions and constraints, linear programming intuition, local vs global optima, convexity's importance, gradient descent conceptually, integer programming difficulty, real allocation problems.

subtopics.title: Logic & proof techniques
subtopics.brief: Mathematical reasoning: if-then statements and converses, necessary vs sufficient, contrapositive, proof by contradiction, counterexamples, quantifiers, common logical errors in arguments.

subtopics.title: Cognitive biases
subtopics.brief: Judgment biases: confirmation bias, availability heuristic, anchoring, overconfidence calibration, hindsight bias, survivorship bias, Dunning-Kruger honestly, debiasing techniques that actually work. Kahneman-grounded.

subtopics.title: Decision science
subtopics.brief: Deciding well: expected value thinking, decision trees, reversible vs irreversible decisions, optionality, pre-mortems, decision fatigue, group decision pathologies (groupthink), process vs outcome quality.

subtopics.title: Learning how to learn
subtopics.brief: Evidence-based learning: spaced repetition, retrieval practice vs rereading, interleaving, desirable difficulties, transfer limits, learning styles myth, deliberate practice conditions, metacognition.

subtopics.title: Motivation & habits
subtopics.brief: Behavior change: habit loops (cue/routine/reward), implementation intentions, intrinsic vs extrinsic motivation evidence, goal gradient, streaks psychology, willpower research honestly, environment design.

subtopics.title: Social psychology
subtopics.brief: Social influence: conformity and obedience classics (with replication caveats), persuasion principles, in-group bias, attribution errors, bystander effect, social proof in products, replication crisis awareness.
